message:
feat(runtime, tls): add blocking pool and TLS certificate pinning

Blocking pool (bd-32ck):
- Add BlockingPool with configurable min/max threads and dynamic scaling
- FIFO work queue with soft cancellation support
- Wire into RuntimeBuilder/Runtime/RuntimeHandle with spawn_blocking() API
- Graceful shutdown with bounded drain timeout
- 12 unit tests for pool mechanics and integration

TLS certificates (bd-241f):
- Enhanced error types: CertificateExpired, CertificateNotYetValid,
  ChainValidation, PinMismatch
- Add extend_from_native_roots() for platform root certificates
- Certificate pinning with CertificatePin (SPKI/cert SHA-256) and
  CertificatePinSet
- Add base64 and x509-parser dependencies for pin computation
- 19 unit tests for pinning, chain operations, and error handling

Also includes fixes from concurrent development:
- TraceBufferHandle migration in lab runtime
- RuntimeState Debug impl for non-Debug MetricsProvider
- Three-lane scheduler enhancements

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1cww","title":"Fix hanging test: sync::notify::tests::notify_one_wakes_waiter","description":"The test notify_one_wakes_waiter hangs indefinitely. The test uses thread::spawn with a 50ms sleep before calling notify_one(). After joining the thread, the second poll should return Ready but it appears the test hangs after the first poll returns Pending correctly.

Investigation shows:
1. First poll registers waiter with noop_waker, returns Pending ✓
2. Thread sleeps 50ms, calls notify_one()
3. notify_one() sets notified=true, takes waker, calls wake() (noop)
4. Main thread joins (wait for thread completion)
5. Second poll checks notified flag - should return Ready

Needs investigation into why the test hangs at step 4 or 5.","notes":"Fixed deadlock in Notified::poll Waiting state - was calling cleanup() while holding waiters lock, but cleanup() also tries to acquire the lock. Restructured to release lock before calling cleanup.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-26T16:18:50.749670010Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T16:24:48.766650557Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T16:24:48.766588981Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-1fej","title":"Complete reactor waker registration","description":"Goal: complete reactor waker registration and readiness notifications. Ensure correct edge/level behavior with cancellation safety. Include unit tests for register/deregister, spurious wakeups, and readiness transitions.","notes":"Migrated UnixListener and UnixStream from Registration (stub) to IoRegistration (working).

Key changes:
- UnixListener: Changed from eagerly failing Cx::register_io() to lazy IoRegistration via Cx::current().io_driver_handle().register()
- UnixStream: Same pattern - lazy registration with register_interest_for_read/write helpers
- Both now use Mutex<Option<IoRegistration>> for interior mutability
- Removed unused UnixListenerSource/UnixStreamSource wrapper types
- Updated split.rs for new from_parts signature
- All 3118 tests pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:31:14.666033983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T01:56:06.459021702Z","closed_at":"2026-01-31T01:56:06.458882854Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io","reactor","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fej","depends_on_id":"bd-1j64","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:31:14.685778774Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Creating follow-up bead for test fixes.","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:49:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-221m","title":"Service Layer Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E, middleware)","description":"# Service Layer Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for the service layer (lnm, tower equivalent) ensuring composability, cancel-correctness, and middleware stack behavior.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- Service trait: call, poll_ready
- Layer trait: composition
- ServiceBuilder: layer chaining
- Standard middleware: timeout, rate limit, retry, concurrency limit

### 2. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Basic service call | Request/response lifecycle |
| Timeout middleware | Budget integration, timeout enforcement |
| Rate limiting | Request throttling, backpressure |
| Retry with backoff | Exponential backoff, max attempts |
| Circuit breaker | Open/half-open/closed states |
| Bulkhead isolation | Resource partitioning |
| Middleware stack | Correct layering order |

### 3. Cancel-Safety Tests
- Cancel mid-request: clean abort
- Cancel with pending retries: no orphan requests
- Cancel with rate limit queue: queue cleanup

### 4. Load Tests
- Concurrent requests
- Backpressure behavior
- Resource exhaustion handling

## Logging Requirements
- Middleware events logged with request IDs
- On failure: dump middleware stack state
- Request/response timings logged

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All middleware have unit tests
- [ ] E2E scenarios pass with real HTTP backend
- [ ] Cancel-safety verified for all middleware
- [ ] Load tests pass without resource leaks
- [ ] `cargo test service` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib service
cargo test --test service_e2e
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"PinkMountain","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:47:11.707559049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T07:17:19.182769279Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T07:17:19.182686816Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-221m","depends_on_id":"asupersync-lnm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-22vb","title":"Codec Framework Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E, framing)","description":"# Codec Framework Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for the codec framework (4nz, tokio-util codecs equivalent) ensuring correct encoding/decoding and framing.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- Decoder trait: decode, decode_eof
- Encoder trait: encode
- FramedRead: framed decoding
- FramedWrite: framed encoding
- Framed: bidirectional codec
- Common codecs: LinesCodec, BytesCodec, LengthDelimitedCodec

### 2. Codec Implementation Tests
- Lines: newline delimiter
- Bytes: raw passthrough
- LengthDelimited: length-prefixed frames
- JSON: serde_json codec
- Custom codec patterns

### 3. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Line-delimited protocol | Text protocol parsing |
| Length-prefixed frames | Binary protocol framing |
| Partial frame handling | Buffer management |
| Large frame handling | Memory efficiency |
| Cancel mid-frame | Clean abort |
| Codec chaining | Nested framing |
| Error propagation | Decode error handling |

### 4. Edge Cases
- Empty frames
- Maximum frame size
- Incomplete frames at EOF
- Malformed input
- Concurrent encode/decode

### 5. Performance Tests
- Throughput for various codecs
- Memory allocation per frame
- Zero-copy where possible

## Logging Requirements
- Codec events logged with frame sizes
- On failure: dump buffer state, codec state
- Frame metrics: encoded, decoded, errors

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All codecs have unit tests
- [ ] E2E scenarios pass
- [ ] Edge cases handled correctly
- [ ] Performance baselines established
- [ ] `cargo test codec` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib codec
cargo test --test codec_e2e
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"FrostyCanyon","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:49:24.986532463Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T04:13:36.984847672Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T04:13:36.984770449Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met. 38 codec unit tests (LinesCodec, LengthDelimitedCodec, grpc codec, frame codec), 33 E2E tests (line-delimited protocol, length-prefixed frames, partial frame handling, large frames, empty frames, unicode, max length, CRLF, multi-frame, encode/decode symmetry, buffer state preservation). Edge cases: empty frames, max frame size, incomplete at EOF, malformed input. All 71 tests pass with 0 failures.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22vb","depends_on_id":"asupersync-4nz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-hszn","title":"gRPC Framework Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E, streaming)","description":"# gRPC Framework Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for the gRPC framework (3or, tonic equivalent) ensuring protocol compliance, streaming correctness, and cancel-safety.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- Service definitions: codegen output
- Request/Response types: encoding, decoding
- Metadata: headers, trailers
- Status codes: mapping, propagation
- Interceptors: client and server

### 2. Streaming Pattern Tests
- Unary: single request, single response
- Server streaming: single request, stream response
- Client streaming: stream request, single response
- Bidirectional: stream request, stream response

### 3. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Unary RPC | Basic request/response |
| Server streaming | Large result set |
| Client streaming | Upload pattern |
| Bidirectional chat | Real-time communication |
| Deadline propagation | Timeout inheritance |
| Cancel mid-stream | Clean termination |
| Metadata round-trip | Header/trailer handling |
| Reflection service | Service discovery |

### 4. Protocol Compliance Tests
- gRPC over HTTP/2
- Message framing
- Compression: gzip, deflate, none
- Content-Type validation
- Status code mapping

### 5. Load Tests
- Concurrent RPCs
- Long-running streams
- Connection multiplexing
- Backpressure handling

### 6. Security Tests
- TLS integration
- mTLS client auth
- Token validation

## Logging Requirements
- RPC events logged with method names
- On failure: dump message state, stream state
- Timing: request/response latency per RPC

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All streaming patterns have tests
- [ ] Protocol compliance verified
- [ ] Cancel-safety for all patterns
- [ ] Load tests pass without leaks
- [ ] `cargo test grpc` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib grpc
cargo test --test grpc_e2e
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"PinkMountain","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:49:43.146586417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T07:24:11.064971182Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T07:24:11.064895852Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hszn","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3or","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}

## Purpose
Map n5o (Tokio-runtime equivalent) sub-tasks to xrc (Phase 1) hierarchy.

- 8z9: spawn variants
- ior: thread-safe region
- 9d3: work-stealing
- c61: runtime config

- [ ] n5o depends on xrc (n5o is alias, xrc is canonical)
- [ ] n5o description notes it's a tokio-equivalence alias
- [ ] xrc priority updated to P1 (was P2)
- [ ] All xrc.* sub-tasks have matching priorities
- [ ] `br show asupersync-xrc` shows correct hierarchy

## Verification
```bash
br show asupersync-n5o  # Should depend on xrc
br show asupersync-xrc  # Should be P1 with full sub-task tree
```","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ClaudeOpus45","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:28:21.066247555Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T03:15:02.103198644Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:34:21.037258253Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hzrb","depends_on_id":"asupersync-n5o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-hzrb","depends_on_id":"asupersync-xrc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-hzrb","depends_on_id":"bd-zs64","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":12,"issue_id":"bd-hzrb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Checked xrc.* priorities: 7 items are non-P1 (xrc.1.4 P3; xrc.5 / xrc.5.1/.2/.3 P3; xrc.6 / xrc.6.1 P4).  {"id":"bd-zs64","title":"[EPIC-INFRA] Bead Harmonization - API & Agent Interface Coherence","description":"# [EPIC-INFRA] Bead Harmonization - API & Agent Interface Coherence

## Purpose
Master epic tracking bead harmonization: merge duplicates, standardize naming, fix priorities, migrate orphan sub-tasks. NO FEATURE LOSS.

## Sub-Tasks
1. bd-q0om: Merge I/O EPICs (90l9 → ds8)
2. bd-hzrb: Merge Parallel Runtime EPICs (n5o → xrc)
3. bd-kh65: Standardize EPIC naming convention
4. bd-4rpn: Fix priority alignments
5. bd-3p6e: Migrate orphan sub-tasks to hierarchical IDs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All duplicate EPICs merged or closed with migration notes
- [ ] All EPICs follow [EPIC-{category}] naming convention
- [ ] Priority follows dependency order (parents ≤ children)
- [ ] All sub-tasks use hierarchical IDs ({epic}.{n}.{m})
- [ ] No cycles in dependency graph (verified via `bv --robot-insights | jq '.Cycles'`)
- [ ] `bv --robot-triage` returns healthy project metrics

## Verification Commands
```bash
bv --robot-triage | jq '.quick_ref'
bv --robot-insights | jq '.Cycles'
br list --status=closed | grep "DUPLICATE\|MIGRATED"
```

## Logging Strategy
- All changes logged in docs/bead-harmonization-migration.md
- Each bead update includes reason in description
- Git commit messages reference bead IDs","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:27:46.833876766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T03:15:50.988353887Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T03:15:13.406855498Z","close_reason":"All harmonization sub-tasks closed; naming/priority alignment verified; orphans migrated; no dependency cycles","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-zs64","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Checked sub-tasks: bd-q0om/bd-hzrb/bd-kh65/bd-4rpn/bd-3p6e all closed. Verified xrc/n5o hierarchy, epic naming consistent, priorities aligned (per bd-4rpn close_reason), and br dep cycles empty.","created_at":"2026-01-23T03:15:07Z"},{"id":14,"issue_id":"bd-zs64","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Ran bv --robot-insights | jq '.Cycles' => null (no cycles). Orphan migration (bd-3p6e) verified and logged in docs/bead-harmonization-migration.md.","created_at":"2026-01-23T03:15:50Z"}]}

@@ -11,8 +11,31 @@ pub enum TlsError {
     InvalidDnsName(String),
     /// TLS handshake failure.
     Handshake(String),
-    /// Certificate error.
+    /// Certificate error (generic).
     Certificate(String),
+    /// Certificate has expired.
+    CertificateExpired {
+        /// The time the certificate expired (Unix timestamp in seconds).
+        expired_at: i64,
+        /// Description of the certificate.
+        description: String,
+    },
+    /// Certificate is not yet valid.
+    CertificateNotYetValid {
+        /// The time the certificate becomes valid (Unix timestamp in seconds).
+        valid_from: i64,
+        /// Description of the certificate.
+        description: String,
+    },
+    /// Certificate chain validation failed.
+    ChainValidation(String),
+    /// Certificate pin mismatch.
+    PinMismatch {
+        /// Expected pin(s).
+        expected: Vec<String>,
+        /// Actual pin found.
+        actual: String,
+    },
     /// Configuration error.
     Configuration(String),
     /// I/O error during TLS operations.
@@ -30,6 +53,19 @@ impl fmt::Display for TlsError {
             Self::InvalidDnsName(name) => write!(f, "invalid DNS name: {name}"),
             Self::Handshake(msg) => write!(f, "TLS handshake failed: {msg}"),
             Self::Certificate(msg) => write!(f, "certificate error: {msg}"),
+            Self::CertificateExpired {
+                expired_at,
+                description,
+            } => write!(f, "certificate expired at {expired_at}: {description}"),
+            Self::CertificateNotYetValid {
+                valid_from,
+                description,
+            } => write!(f, "certificate not valid until {valid_from}: {description}"),
+            Self::ChainValidation(msg) => write!(f, "certificate chain validation failed: {msg}"),
+            Self::PinMismatch { expected, actual } => write!(
+                f,
+                "certificate pin mismatch: expected one of {expected:?}, got {actual}"
+            ),
             Self::Configuration(msg) => write!(f, "TLS configuration error: {msg}"),
             Self::Io(err) => write!(f, "I/O error: {err}"),
             Self::Timeout(duration) => write!(f, "TLS operation timed out after {duration:?}"),
